title: 2026 California gubernatorial election
description: California’s 2026 gubernatorial race is for governor, the state’s top executive office. Because Gov. Gavin Newsom is term-limited, the race is an open seat, and California’s top-two primary system means the June primary will determine which two candidates advance to November. The contest is drawing attention because California is heavily Democratic overall, but the field is crowded and recent polling has shown multiple candidates with plausible paths to the top two. Key contrasts in the race include public safety and cost-of-living messaging from Republicans Chad Bianco and Steve Hilton, versus Democratic candidates emphasizing affordability, consumer protection, and opposition to Donald Trump.

candidates.name: Chad Bianco
candidates.summary: Chad Bianco is the Riverside County sheriff and a Republican candidate for governor. His campaign has emphasized public safety, lower taxes, and reducing regulation, and Ballotpedia lists him as a candidate in California’s June 2, 2026 top-two primary. He is running as a law-and-order conservative with support from some Republican elected officials and law-enforcement groups.

summary_sources.title: Sheriff Chad Bianco | Riverside County Sheriff, CA
Ché Ahn California Governor | Official Campaign Website

career_history.title: Sheriff-Coroner-Public Administrator
career_history.description: Elected county sheriff; oversees the Riverside County Sheriff’s Department.

career_history.title: Deputy / law enforcement officer
career_history.description: Worked in the sheriff’s department for about 26 years before becoming sheriff.

candidates.name: Steve Hilton
candidates.summary: Steve Hilton is a Republican candidate for governor and a former political adviser and media commentator. His campaign site and Ballotpedia profile describe him as a conservative candidate focused on housing, taxes, education, and making California more business-friendly. He is running as an outsider with prior experience in U.K. politics and U.S. media.

summary_sources.title: Steve Hilton for California Governor | Official Campaign Site

career_history.description: Served as a senior adviser in the Cameron government.

career_history.description: Worked as a media commentator and contributor.

career_history.title: Entrepreneur / broadcaster
career_history.description: Built a career in media, commentary, and entrepreneurship.

candidates.name: Eric Swalwell
candidates.summary: Eric Swalwell is a Democratic member of Congress from California’s 14th District and a candidate for governor. His background includes service as a prosecutor and city councilmember, and his campaign has framed the race around affordability, public safety, and opposition to Donald Trump. He is one of several high-profile Democrats in a crowded top-two primary.

candidates.name: Katie Porter
candidates.summary: Katie Porter is a former U.S. representative from Orange County and a Democratic candidate for governor. Her campaign and biography emphasize consumer protection, affordability, and a confrontational style toward corporate and political power. She is running as a progressive-leaning Democrat with a record in Congress and as a law professor.

candidates.name: Antonio Villaraigosa
candidates.summary: Antonio Villaraigosa is a former Los Angeles mayor and former speaker of the California State Assembly who is running for governor as a Democrat. His campaign and biography stress experience, problem-solving, and a focus on affordability, jobs, and public services. He is one of the most experienced candidates in the field and is seeking a return to statewide office after earlier runs for governor.

summary_sources.title: About Antonio Villaraigosa — Antonio Villaraigosa for Governor 2026

candidates.name: Xavier Becerra
candidates.summary: Xavier Becerra is a former U.S. secretary of health and human services, former California attorney general, and former longtime member of Congress. His campaign presents him as a defender of the California Dream, and his background includes decades in state and federal office. He is running as a high-profile Democrat with extensive executive and legislative experience.
